name: a future fact the model wrongly marked asserted is normalized to expected, then becomes occurred after the date passes
description: Adversarial: note A is captured 2026-06-12 about a concert on 2026-06-20 but the model (lapse) emits assertion='asserted' for a still-future event. normalize_future_assertion (analysis.extraction) catches valid_from > captured_at and relaxes it to 'expected' — a future event must not read as already-happened (docs/ANALYSIS.md 'Temporal model': future-tense facts carry expected). Note B is captured 2026-06-21, AFTER the date passed, restating the SAME concert/instant as occurred (assertion='asserted', valid_from now in the past relative to capture, so normalize leaves it asserted). Different value_json (status expected->occurred) at equal valid_from => state newest-wins: occurred supersedes expected, one active asserted fact remains. Pins that capture-relative future detection flips a mislabeled assertion and that the expected row does not linger active after the event occurs.

steps.body: Ingrid is going to the Sigrid concert on June 20th.

facts.statement: Ingrid attends the Sigrid concert on 2026-06-20.

steps.body: Ingrid went to the Sigrid concert last night, it was great.

facts.statement: Ingrid attended the Sigrid concert on 2026-06-20.
